metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting activities for your 3-year-old in Hackney and beyond! From free museum adventures with hands-on exhibits to delightful theatre shows designed for little ones, there's plenty to spark their imagination and keep them entertained. I've also included some options further afield that are worth the journey.

activities.title: Young V&A Museum
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Road, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
activities.description: The Young V&A is a 'doing' museum with interactive spaces and activities, including sensory playscapes, an 'Imagination Playground' construction zone, and a story-telling stage. It showcases 2,000 objects from the museum's collection of art, design, and performance.
activities.whyItWorks: Created with and for kids, the museum has hands-on exhibits in the Play Gallery and a performance stage to spark imagination. There is a family-friendly cafe with bottle warming facilities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from there.
transport.tip: Check out the Mini Museum space, designed for under 2s with sensory experiences.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: This centre celebrates children's books, bringing them to life in an interactive setting. Enjoy immersive play spaces known as Story Worlds, and the Story Garden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: There are two floors of immersive play space and a Story Garden playground. The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford. It's a short walk from there.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific events and age recommendations.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: The playground is designed specifically for toddlers. The Olympic Park offers a massive outdoor space for exploring.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford. It's a short walk from there.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the Westfield Shopping Centre for baby facilities.

activities.title: 'Driving' the DLR
activities.description: Ride the driverless DLR and sit at the front, pretending to drive the train!
activities.whyItWorks: Toddlers can sit where a driver would be. It's an adventure on public transport.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to a DLR interchange station like Stratford or Canary Wharf.
transport.tip: Start at Tower Gateway for easy access from Hackney.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Discover the story of London as a great port, with exhibits charting history from Roman times to the present.
activities.whyItWorks: There is the Mudlarks soft play area, a dedicated gallery for children under 8. There are various family trails around the museum for children to interact with.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the DLR to West India Quay.
transport.tip: Look out for the special red signs explaining things in a child-friendly way.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore the vast parklands, playgrounds, and waterways of the Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Vast outdoor space. There are playgrounds and waterways to explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the park's website for events and activities.

activities.title: Little Angel Theatre
activities.description: This theatre is a gem for this age group, with puppet shows adapted from picture books running about 45-50 minutes.
activities.whyItWorks: The shows are built for ages 2-5. The intimate space means your toddler won't feel lost.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Islington, or the tube to Angel.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific show dates and booking information.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Meet farmyard animals in the heart of Hackney.
activities.whyItWorks: Get up close with animals. It is easily accessible.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, cycle or take a short bus ride.
transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops on their website.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Explore sea travel, exploration, trade and naval history. The AHOY! gallery is designed for under 5s.
activities.whyItWorks: The AHOY! gallery is a dedicated space for under 5s. The exhibits have child-friendly activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Greenwich.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Greenwich Park.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.address: 100 London Road, Forest Hill, London SE23 3PQ
activities.price: Free (Aquarium and Butterfly House have a fee)
activities.description: Explore natural history displays, musical instruments, and gardens. Visit the Animal Walk to see goats, sheep and alpacas.
activities.whyItWorks: There is a hands-on music room with instruments to play. The Animal Walk is great for younger kids.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Visit on a Saturday afternoon for family craft activities.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 5BD
activities.description: Discover dinosaurs, fascinating specimens from the animal and plant kingdoms, and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of exhibits are set up for kids. There are interactive displays which help children understand and appreciate the natural world.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: It can get crowded, so try to visit during off-peak times.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits and galleries dedicated to space exploration.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for young children. The Pattern Pod is great for light exploration, sensory and interactive fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book a free entrance ticket in advance.

activities.title: V&A Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2RL
activities.description: Wander through treasure-filled halls from around the world, spanning centuries of art, design, and culture.
activities.whyItWorks: The V&A hosts a range of free activities for families to enjoy every day. There are hands-on family events and performances during the school holidays.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Pick up a free family activity pack from the Learning Centre.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Visit a small zoo in the Hampstead Heath area, home to rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: It's completely free to enter. There are rare species of birds and animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath, then a bus to Golders Hill Park.
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Enjoy a scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: There are lush gardens, a boating lake and playgrounds. It is a spacious park near Battersea Power Station.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a bus to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy by the lake.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animal viewing with a fun egg hunt. The zoo offers a variety of animals to see.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Regent's Park, or the tube to Camden Town.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to save money.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for ages 3-12. Combines exploring the palace gardens with a fun Easter activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hampton Court from Waterloo.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to guarantee entry.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines indoor and outdoor play with an Easter theme. There are animals to meet.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Hatton Cross, then a bus to Hobbledown Heath.
transport.tip: Plan for a full day to explore everything the park has to offer.

activities.title: The Lion Inside
activities.description: A theatre show suitable for ages 3-7 years.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for a specific age group. It offers a theatre experience for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to London Bridge.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific show dates and booking information.

activities.title: Planet Omar
activities.description: A theatre show suitable for ages 7+ years.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for a specific age group. It offers a theatre experience for children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to London Bridge.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific show dates and booking information.

activities.title: Hampton Court Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Hampton Court Palace offers a Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt, leading families around the historic grounds. The Magic Garden playground is also open.
activities.whyItWorks: The Magic Garden playground is open. This is a themed Easter trail.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hampton Court from Waterloo.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to guarantee entry.

activities.title: Imagine Festival
activities.description: Southbank Centre's annual Imagine Festival is the UK's leading arts festival for children aged 0–11 years and their grown-ups. The festival's multi-artform programme presents music, storytelling, dance, crafts, comedy and more.
activities.whyItWorks: The festival has events for all ages. There are lots of free activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Study the programme beforehand and make bookings so you don't miss out.

activities.title: Architecture Explorers: Family Trail
activities.description: What will you discover on our interactive family activity trail? Grab a map and let's find out!
activities.whyItWorks: An interactive family activity trail. It is a free event.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific dates and times.
